ruling_text: You may play those cards that turn even if Harnfel leaves the battlefield or another player gains control of it.

name: Birgi, God of Storytelling // Harnfel, Horn of Bounty

name: Birgi, God of Storytelling // Harnfel, Horn of Bounty
ruling_text: Any cards exiled with Harnfel that you don’t play remain exiled.

ruling_text: Snow is a supertype, not a card type. It has no rules meaning or function by itself, but spells and abilities may refer to it.

name: Jorn, God of Winter // Kaldring, the Rimestaff
ruling_text: The {S} symbol is a generic mana symbol. It represents a cost that can be paid by one mana that was produced by a snow source. That mana can be any color or colorless.

name: Jorn, God of Winter // Kaldring, the Rimestaff
ruling_text: Snow isn’t a type of mana. If an effect says you may spend mana as though it were any type, you can’t pay for {S} using mana that wasn’t produced by a snow source.

name: Jorn, God of Winter // Kaldring, the Rimestaff

name: Tergrid, God of Fright // Tergrid's Lantern
ruling_text: A permanent card is an artifact, creature, enchantment, land, or planeswalker card.

name: Tergrid, God of Fright // Tergrid's Lantern
ruling_text: If an opponent sacrifices a nontoken permanent, it doesn’t matter which graveyard it goes to. Tergrid’s ability will trigger.

name: Tergrid, God of Fright // Tergrid's Lantern
ruling_text: If the card leaves the graveyard before Tergrid’s ability resolves, you can’t put it onto the battlefield, even if it returns to the graveyard before the ability resolves.

name: Tergrid, God of Fright // Tergrid's Lantern

name: Tergrid, God of Fright // Tergrid's Lantern

name: Tergrid, God of Fright // Tergrid's Lantern

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer
ruling_text: A planeswalker is dealt excess damage if it’s dealt damage greater than its current loyalty.

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer
ruling_text: It doesn’t matter if a creature or planeswalker an opponent controls was dealt combat damage earlier in the turn. The only thing that matters is that the damage dealt to that permanent that caused excess damage to be dealt was noncombat damage.

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer
ruling_text: Toralf’s ability doesn’t affect the damage dealt to the creature or planeswalker an opponent controls. That damage will still be dealt as normal.

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer
ruling_text: The damage Toralf deals because of its triggered ability is noncombat damage. That damage may cause Toralf’s ability to trigger again.

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer
ruling_text: If the target is an illegal target as the ability tries to resolve, the ability won’t resolve and none of its effects will happen. You won’t return Toralf’s Hammer to its owner’s hand.

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer
ruling_text: You unattach Toralf’s Hammer as part of the cost of activating the ability. If the ability doesn’t resolve, Toralf’s Hammer doesn’t become reattached.

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer
ruling_text: There is a single triangle icon in the top left corner of the front face. There is a double triangle icon in the top left corner of the back face.

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer
ruling_text: To determine whether it is legal to play a modal double-faced card, consider only the characteristics of the face you’re playing and ignore the other face’s characteristics.

name: Toralf, God of Fury // Toralf's Hammer

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor
ruling_text: If Valki leaves the battlefield before its enters-the-battlefield ability resolves, each opponent will reveal their hand, but no cards will be exiled.

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor
ruling_text: You don’t choose which creature card exiled with Valki that Valki will become a copy of until that ability is resolving. (In many cases, the value you chose for X will give away your intentions.)

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor
ruling_text: If there are no creature cards exiled with Valki with converted mana cost equal to the value of X as Valki’s activated ability resolves, nothing happens. God of Lies, indeed.

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor
ruling_text: If Valki becomes a copy of a creature during the same turn Valki enters the battlefield, you can’t attack with him or use any {T} abilities he gains.

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor
ruling_text: Valki copies the printed values of the exiled creature card. Notably, once Valki becomes a copy of another creature card, he won’t have his own printed activated ability.

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor
ruling_text: You can activate Valki’s ability multiple times in response to one another. This may briefly allow Valki to copy different creature cards. You’ll get priority to cast spells or activate abilities in between each of Valki’s activated abilities.

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor
ruling_text: If an effect begins to apply to Valki before it becomes a copy, that effect will continue to apply.

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor
ruling_text: If another object becomes a copy of Valki, it will become whatever Valki is copying. That object remains a copy even if Valki leaves the battlefield.

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor

name: Valki, God of Lies // Tibalt, Cosmic Impostor
